Assay · all projects 2 sessions · 3 active days
01 Adoption & Usage Breadth
STRONG

Breadth and growth show how far AI usage has spread across projects, not how good any of it is -- a narrow or flat trend is a cue to invest in onboarding, not a mark against anyone.

2 sessions
3 active days
2 projects
0.7 sessions/active-day
0 dormant projects 1 tools unused
+40724% week-over-week AI lines 20412 recent vs 50 prior

Usage is broad and trending up week over week.

02 Cache Hygiene
WATCH Prov.

High cache reuse means repeated context is served cheaply from cache instead of re-billed as fresh input. It is a cost signal, not a quality one -- a big one-shot task legitimately shows low reuse, and vendor cache lifetimes are invisible here.

0% cache-read share of billed input
0 cache reads
0 cache writes

High reuse is cheaper, not better work -- a large one-shot task legitimately shows low reuse.

Vendor cache lifetimes (TTLs) are invisible, so this is a day-grain approximation.

Little input is served from cache this window -- expected for one-shot or exploratory work.

03 Context Health
WATCH

Frequent compaction means sessions are outgrowing their context window mid-task -- worth trying shorter, more focused sessions rather than reading it as a quality problem.

2 sessions
6 median turns
35000 tokens peak context
18 min median active work code sessions: ~18 min
0% compaction rate
100% code sessions 0% conversational

Compaction is rare so far, but too few sessions this window to call context health confidently.

04 Coverage & Confidence
SOLID Prov.

Coverage is the honesty backbone -- it says how much of every other figure rests on complete data. Low activity coverage means line and edit signals cover only part of your usage; low priced coverage means some cost is excluded, never a real zero.

100% activity coverage lines/edits captured
100% priced coverage cost known
0% cost-only tokens no line signals
  • claude-code >99%
  • codex <1%

Cost-only tools (Gemini CLI, Cline, plugins) contribute tokens and cost but no line or edit signals -- see ROADMAP.

Per-record granularity (turn vs session) is not yet surfaced, so a mix isn't flagged here.

Most usage carries full activity and price data -- the other figures rest on solid coverage.

05 Model Fit
WATCH Prov.

High premium-model share isn't wrong, but routine edits and boilerplate are often just as good on cheaper models -- a place to trim spend without losing output.

98.8% premium (>=$20/1M out) 6386.7 lines/1M tok
1.2% cheaper (<$20/1M out) 5298857.1 lines/1M tok
2.5% sub-agent delegation of tokens run inside Task sub-agents
~$132/mo est. savings (upper bound) premium-tier tokens repriced on claude-sonnet-4-5
  • claude-opus-4-5 300000 tokens
  • claude-sonnet-4-5 3500 tokens

Est. savings is an upper bound -- it assumes every premium-tier token could move to claude-sonnet-4-5 with no quality loss (rarely true) and projects your active-day pace to 30 days; directional, a prompt to review, not a recommendation to switch.

Nearly all tokens run on premium models -- consider delegating routine work to cheaper models or sub-agents.

06 Model Right-Sizing
—

A premium-model turn that emits only a handful of output tokens is often boilerplate or a quick answer a cheaper model handles as well. On a flat plan this is about speed and rate limits, not dollars. Task difficulty is invisible, so this is a prompt to look at a few, never a verdict.

No premium-model turns in this window.

07 Reasoning Share
LEAN Prov.

Reasoning tokens are billed as output. A high share means much of the model's work is internal deliberation, which can be overkill on routine tasks. Only some tools report reasoning (Codex and Gemini CLI today), so the coverage figure says how much of your output this even covers.

0% reasoning share of reporting output
0 reasoning tokens
<1% reporting coverage output from tools that report it

Only Codex and Gemini CLI report reasoning tokens; Claude Code doesn't, so this covers part of your output.

Reasoning is a subset of output (already billed there); this is a composition signal, not extra cost.

Reasoning is a modest share of reported output -- the thinking budget looks proportionate.

08 Rework & Rejection
LOW Prov.

Elevated rework or rejection flags friction worth a closer look at specific sessions, but the link between AI churn and real bugs is still contested, so treat it as a lead, not a verdict.

0% rework 0 lines, within-session thrash proxy
3% rejection rate 1 of 35 tool calls declined

Evidence on AI churn's real-world impact is contested; bug/survival impact needs the server stage.

Rework and rejection are both low.

09 Session Taxonomy
— Prov.

Conversational sessions are real work -- design, debugging, and planning rarely touch files -- so a high conversational share is not waste. This is a mix, not a scorecard: read it to understand how you use AI, not to push every session toward edits.

0% conversational no file edits
100% light-edit 1-10 edits
0% heavy-edit >10 edits
  • conversational 0 (0%)
  • light-edit 2 (100%)
  • heavy-edit 0 (0%)

Conversational sessions are real work (design, debugging, planning) -- not idle time.

A thrash bucket needs per-session rework, which isn't stored yet, so it isn't split out here.

Too few sessions this window to characterize the mix.

10 Subscription Fit
SET PLAN Prov.

This projects your window's API-equivalent cost to a month at your active-day pace, then compares it against the flat plan price you configured. A high multiple means the plan is a bargain at your volume; below 1x means API pay-as-you-go might be cheaper. The API figure is an estimate at public prices, not your actual bill.

~$165/mo API-equivalent projected estimate

The API-equivalent $ is an estimate at public pay-as-you-go prices, not your actual bill.

Set config.pricing.monthly_subscription_cost to see whether your flat plan beats API pricing at this volume.

11 Throughput
RAMPING

Lines added is an output-volume signal, not a quality score -- read the trend alongside rework and model fit before deciding more code means more value.

20462 AI lines total
6820.7 lines/active-day
+40724% week-over-week AI lines 20412 recent vs 50 prior
  • project-ea33135eb0 18496 lines
  • project-39f19de764 1966 lines

AI-line output is ramping up week over week.

12 Turn Efficiency
— Prov.

These are prompting-efficiency signals, not quality. A session that lands an edit in a turn or two is efficient; a long session can be deliberate, careful work. Task size is invisible here, so read it directionally, never as a per-person score.

0% one-shot rate code sessions in <=2 turns
6 median turns per code session
12.8K output/turn median tokens

Task size is invisible in logs, so a low one-shot rate can mean hard problems, not weak prompting -- directional only.

Too few code-producing sessions this window to judge prompting efficiency.

SubpathAI linessessions
subpath-30ff2c3799120001
. (root)64961
Total184961

Cost basis · $17 / last 30 days · $6 per active day · the denominator, not the grade.

Directional assay -- aggregate and pseudonymized by default; per-person requires an explicit opt-in (team mode).

AI-line signals: Claude Code and Codex today -- Gemini CLI and Cline contribute cost but not line counts (see ROADMAP).

Quality signals (survival in main, bug impact) need git/issue correlation -- server stage.

Cost is an estimate at public pay-as-you-go API prices -- not your actual spend; subscription plans bill a flat rate and differ.

Project names pseudonymized for sharing -- run with --no-anonymize for real names.
